### 🟢 Cluster Formation (`18:53:52–18:53:57`)
- Member started and joined cluster with 3 voters:

- Both peers marked **inactive** repeatedly

- Peers eventually became reachable (~`18:53:57`), suggesting a **transient DNS/headless service propagation delay** at startup

6. **Update logger flag** — replace `--logger=capnslog` with `--logger=zap`

kaResponse.analysis: All 3 etcd members have ~99.99% DB fragmentation (300MB physical file, only 24KB live data) because large 500KB key writes followed by compaction were never followed by defragmentation, leaving the backend B-tree holding ~300MB of free-but-unreleased pages.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: All 3 etcd members have ~99.99% DB fragmentation (300MB physical file, only 24KB live data) because large 500KB key writes followed by compaction were never followed by defragmentation, leaving the backend B-tree holding ~300MB of free-but-unreleased pages.
causalChain: Signal: EtcdHighFragmentationRatio fires — all 3 etcd members show fragmentation ratio of ~99.99%
Why? etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes is ~300MB but etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_use_in_bytes is only 24KB — the physical DB file is almost entirely free pages
Why? Auto-compaction ran (revision 486) and freed logical MVCC entries, but etcd's bbolt backend does not release physical disk pages to the OS on compaction alone
Why? No defragmentation has ever been performed — etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0, no defrag log entries, and defrag is not configured in the StatefulSet

Root cause: The etcd StatefulSet has no defragmentation mechanism configured. After large 500KB writes to /demo/fragmentation/* keys and subsequent compaction, the physical bbolt DB pages remain allocated and fragmented until an explicit etcdctl defrag is run against each member.

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting StatefulSet/etcd because its spec needs a defragmentation sidecar or init job added, and its memory limits need increasing. The StatefulSet owns all 3 etcd pods and is the correct remediation point. The Service etcd-client is just the signal reporter.
dueDiligence.proportionality: All 3 etcd members are affected equally (~99.99% fragmentation). The StatefulSet is the single resource that controls all members. Defragmentation must be run per-member but the root configuration gap (no defrag job/sidecar) is in the StatefulSet definition.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: all 3 etcd pods (status, logs, previous logs, events), StatefulSet spec, Services, Prometheus fragmentation metrics, defrag inflight metrics, compaction metrics, alert rule definition. Did not check PVC usage directly but etcd DB size (~300MB) is well within 1Gi PVC. Node conditions not checked but OOMKill was isolated to etcd-0 and has recovered.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Full five-whys chain traced: alert → extreme ratio → physical vs logical space mismatch → compaction without defrag → no defrag configured. Cannot go deeper without etcd source code; the bbolt behavior is well-documented.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: Fragmentation ratio backed by live Prometheus metrics (99.99% on all 3 pods). Large write activity backed by etcd-0 previous logs showing 500KB writes. Compaction confirmed by log entry 'finished scheduled compaction at 486'. Zero defrag confirmed by etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0. OOMKill confirmed by pod status (exit code 137, 512Mi limit). All claims tool-verified.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for StatefulSet/etcd in demo-etcd-defrag (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context). N/A — no previously failed remediations to avoid.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) DB genuinely full with live data — ruled out: in_use_size is only 24KB vs 300MB total. (2) Metric reporting bug — ruled out: all 3 independent pods show identical pattern. (3) Defrag in progress — ruled out: etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0 on all members. (4) Storage quota exhaustion — ruled out: 1Gi PVC with 300MB used is not exhausted.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0. -0.03 for not directly querying PVC usage (minor gap, DB size well within limits). -0.02 for range queries returning empty (likely time window mismatch with metric retention, but instant queries confirmed the current state conclusively). Final: 0.95.

contributingFactors: Large 500KB value writes to /demo/fragmentation/* keyspace rapidly grew the etcd backend DB to ~300MB
Auto-compaction (--auto-compaction-retention=1) ran and freed logical space but did NOT reclaim physical disk pages
No defragmentation was ever performed (etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0, no defrag log entries)
etcd-0 was OOMKilled at 19:04:44Z during peak write load, indicating memory pressure from large value handling
Memory limit of 512Mi is undersized for an etcd instance handling 500KB values with a 2GiB quota-backend-bytes setting
